February 10, 1984 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-028 
In your letter of January 18, 1984, you aSKed for advice 
regarding the reporting of an outstanding loan made by a major 
donor comm~ttee dur~ng 1983 wnicn will in part be repaid and in 
part forg~ven ~n 1984. 
You aSKed if this activity is required to 
be reported if tne person does not qual~fy as a major donor 
comm~ttee ~n 1984. 
A loan forgiveness is included within tne definition of 
"contribution" (Gov. Code Sect~on 82015). 
Therefore, if the loan 
forg~veness is for an amount of $5,000 or more, by forgiving the 
loan in 1984, tne committee will requalify for 1984 as a major 
donor committee (Gov. Code Section 82013(c». 
In our telephone conversat~on of last weeK, I advised you 
tnat tnis activity would not count toward tne $5,000 thresnold to 
qualify as a maJor donor dur~ng 1984. 
However, tne staff has 
reconsidered tnis advice and nas concluded tnat a loan forgiveness 
does, in fact, count toward tne qualif~cation of a maJor donor 
committee and would trigger a campaign filing requirement. 
I nope this nas adequately answered your question. 
Please 
contact me if you nave any furtner questions. 
Sincerely, 
Mary Ann Kvasager 
Pol~tical Reform Consultant 
MAK:Kt 


~1s. M.ary Ann Kvasager 
Muffie Meier 
1801 Century Park East 
Suite 500 
Los Angeles, California 
18 January 1984 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 -
K - Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Ms. Kvasager: 
Per our conversation this morning I am requesting a written 
ruling on the following: 
If a major donor from calander year 1983 still 
has an outstanding loan to a committee that is 
in part in be repayed and in part to be forgiven 
in 1984, when and how are those actions to be 
reported on form 461 in 1984? 
Do the actions have to be reported whether or 
not the major donor qualified as a major donor 
in 1984? 
Please respond to me at the above address. 
90067 
Thank you very much for your attention and time on this matter. 
Sincerely, 
Muffie Meier

sections.question: If a major donor from calendar year 1983 still has an outstanding loan to a committee that is in part to be repayed and in part to be forgiven in 1984, when and how are those actions to be reported on form 461 in 1984? Do the actions have to be reported whether or not the major donor qualified as a major donor in 1984?
sections.conclusion: A loan forgiveness is included within the definition of 'contribution' (Gov. Code Section 82015). Therefore, if the loan forgiveness is for an amount of $5,000 or more, by forgiving the loan in 1984, the committee will requalify for 1984 as a major donor committee (Gov. Code Section 82013(c)). The staff has reconsidered prior telephone advice and concluded that a loan forgiveness does, in fact, count toward the qualification of a major donor committee and would trigger a campaign filing requirement.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether a loan forgiveness from a 1983 major donor committee must be reported in 1984 on Form 461, and whether reporting requirements depend on whether the donor qualifies as a major donor in 1984.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Loan forgiveness qualifies as a contribution and counts toward major donor committee qualification. If the forgiven amount is $5,000 or more in 1984, the committee requalifies as a major donor committee for 1984 and must file accordingly, regardless of 1983 status.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (January 18, 1984) and FPPC response (February 10, 1984). FPPC explicitly reconsiders and corrects prior telephone advice. Minor OCR errors present (e.g., 'aSKed', 'tne' for 'the', 'forg~ven'). No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but content clearly organized with question text and conclusion reasoning.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If a major donor from calendar year 1983 still has an outstanding loan to a committee that is in part to be repayed and in part to be forgiven in 1984, when and how are those actions to be reported on form 461 in 1984? Do the actions have to be reported whether or not the major donor qualified as a major donor in 1984?

CONCLUSION: A loan forgiveness is included within the definition of 'contribution' (Gov. Code Section 82015). Therefore, if the loan forgiveness is for an amount of $5,000 or more, by forgiving the loan in 1984, the committee will requalify for 1984 as a major donor committee (Gov. Code Section 82013(c)). The staff has reconsidered prior telephone advice and concluded that a loan forgiveness does, in fact, count toward the qualification of a major donor committee and would trigger a campaign filing requirement.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-028 addresses whether a loan forgiveness from a 1983 major donor committee triggers major donor status and reporting requirements in 1984. The FPPC concludes that loan forgiveness counts as a contribution and if $5,000 or more, requalifies the committee as a major donor with corresponding filing obligations.
